title: Vanguards of Healthcare: HC9 Ventures on Operator Edge
summary: “We are not a passive capital partner.” Richard Lungen of HC9 Ventures joins fellow co-founder and general partner Charlie Falcone to explain why their venture firm is built around operators, not financiers. The pair sit down with Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Jonathan Palmer for a wide-ranging discussion of the firm’s operator-led approach to venture investing. title: Gas Is Letting Data Centers Grow Beyond the Grid (Podcast)
summary: Artificial intelligence is heating up the race to build data centers, and with BloombergNEF projecting 194 gigawatts of demand by 2035, the biggest challenge isn’t computing power… It’s electricity. Faced with years-long grid connection delays, developers are turning to on-site gas generation to bring projects online faster, creating a rapidly expanding market for turbines, engines, fuel cells and battery storage. title: Crop Prices Hit 3-Year High as Black Sea Attacks, Heat Stoke Food Inflation
summary: Crop prices rose to a three-year high as heat waves and escalating attacks in the Black Sea threatened to disrupt global grain trade, reviving risks to food inflation that have also been stoked by the war in Iran.

title: McCormick Will Add London Listing After Unilever Food Deal
summary: McCormick & Co. has picked London as a secondary listing location, just months after striking a deal to combine with Unilever Plc’s food business and in a blow to Amsterdam’s hopes.

title: Stocks Fall on AI Jitters as Oil Lifts Bond Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell as Alphabet Inc.’s earnings triggered fresh unease over ever-higher artificial intelligence spending, while rising oil prices drove bond yields higher.

title: Alphabet’s Century Bond Has Now Lost a 10th of Its Face Value
summary: A 100-year bond that Alphabet Inc. issued earlier this year in the sterling market as it gorged on debt around the world has dropped below 90 pence on the pound for the first time.

title: Asian Oil Buyers in Talks to Reroute Saudi Red Sea Flows
summary: Some Asian crude oil buyers are in talks with Saudi Aramco to potentially reroute flows around Africa after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea, according to traders familiar with the matter.

title: India May Allow Portfolio Managers to Take Unhedged Short Bets
summary: India’s securities regulator has proposed allowing domestic portfolio management service providers to take short positions in equity derivatives for the first time, easing rules for professional money managers even as it tightens oversight of retail trading.

title: JPMorgan Quants Say Strongest Earnings Upgrades Are in Europe
summary: Europe now leads the world as the region with the sharpest upgrades to corporate earnings estimates from analysts, according to JPMorgan Chase & Co.

title: Kuehne+Nagel Weighs Options For Apex Logistics Unit
summary: Kuehne+Nagel International AG is considering strategic options for its Apex Logistics unit, an Asia-focused freight forwarder, people with knowledge of the matter said.

title: Gold Falls as Middle East Conflict Curbs Dip-Buying Momentum
summary: Gold fell as the escalating conflict in the Middle East continued to push up energy prices, fueling bets the Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

title: Roche Cuts Generic Impact Estimate, Pointing to More Growth
summary: Roche Holding’s results showed a slower-than-expected recovery for eye blockbuster Vabysmo as investors look to a key new drug to power growth ahead.

title: Dangote Reverts to Pricing Gasoline in Naira After Dollar Switch
summary: Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals Fze reverted to the sale of petroleum products in naira, a week after switching to sales in US dollars.

title: AI Debt Competing with Treasuries Is Adding to Lofty US Yields
summary: The US 30-year bond yield has held above 5% for the longest stretch since the start of the financial crisis.

title: Pound Rally Fizzles as Burnham Keeps UK Fiscal Plans Unclear
summary: The pound’s rally is at risk from the new UK government’s still unclear fiscal plans as well as the potential for fewer interest-rate hikes eroding the currency’s high-yield appeal.

title: China Tankers Reach Red Sea Chokepoint Despite Houthi Threat
summary: One China-owned oil tanker loaded with Saudi Arabian crude exited the Red Sea via the Bab el-Mandeb chokepoint, and another was on course to follow, despite overnight Houthi attacks on vessels in the region.

title: Hedge Funds’ Favorite US Bond Trade Is Sputtering
summary: Hedge funds’ most popular trade in the US bond market is showing signs of maxing out.

title: German Bond Yields Hit 15-Year High as Oil Spikes Before ECB
summary: German bond yields rose to the highest level since 2011 as surging energy prices and mounting inflation expectations fueled bets on interest-rate hikes ahead of the European Central Bank’s decision later Thursday.

title: Victoria Plc Wins Support for Debt Deal After HSBC U-Turn
summary: Troubled UK carpetmaker Victoria Plc secured enough bondholder support for a consensual debt restructuring after HSBC Holdings Plc backed the plan at the last minute, averting a costlier, more time-consuming court process.

title: EasyJet Says Customers are Resuming More Normal Booking Patterns
summary: EasyJet Plc said customers are becoming more confident to book longer in advance again after resorting to last-minute purchases out of concern of cancelations, helping the low-cost carrier improve predictability and steady operations.

title: Indonesian Stocks Briefly Near Bull Market as Sentiment Steadies
summary: A brief rally in Indonesian stocks pushed the benchmark index to the cusp of a bull market, as investors rotated into laggards and concerns over the country’s fiscal health eased.

title: BlackRock Says Treasury Yields Offer Solid Buffer Against Losses
summary: US Treasuries are offering investors solid protection against losses as yields remain elevated, according to BlackRock Inc.
